title: Talon Infrastructure Config
description: JSON Schema for talon.config.yaml — infrastructure configuration owned by the DevOps/platform team. Covers LLM provider connections, gateway settings, storage paths, semantic cache, and observability. Agent policy (capabilities, compliance, cost limits) belongs in agent.talon.yaml.

dataTier.description: Data classification tier: 0/public, 1/internal, 2/confidential (ascending sensitivity). String aliases are matched case-insensitively by Talon; this schema lists the lowercase canonical form.

toolPolicyAction.description: filter (default) removes forbidden tools from the request; block rejects the request.

attachmentPolicy.description: Scanning of base64-encoded file attachments embedded in LLM API requests.

gatewayEgressPolicy.description: Egress allow/deny rules keyed on data classification tier and destination provider/region. Supports data-transfer controls; decisions are recorded in signed evidence.

default_action.description: Applied when no rule covers the request's data tier.

allowed_providers.description: Destination provider names; "*" matches any configured provider.

allowed_regions.description: Destination regions (e.g. EU, US, LOCAL) matched against the provider's resolved region. An unknown region never matches (fail-closed).

data_dir.description: Base directory for all state (evidence.db, secrets.db, cache.db, agent state). Default ~/.talon. Env: TALON_DATA_DIR.

secrets_key.description: AES-256 vault encryption key (exactly 32 bytes). Prefer the TALON_SECRETS_KEY env var over putting key material in this file.

signing_key.description: HMAC-SHA256 evidence signing key (>= 32 bytes). Prefer the TALON_SIGNING_KEY env var over putting key material in this file.

default_policy.description: Filename of the agent policy file loaded by default.

agents_dir.description: Directory scanned recursively for agent.talon.yaml files — one file per AI use case (#267). When set it is authoritative for fleet membership (default_policy no longer defines an agent). Empty/absent keeps single-file mode.

agents_reload_interval.description: Cadence of the periodic safe config reload (#269), e.g. "30s". Agent files (single-file or agents_dir) are re-scanned on this interval; valid changes activate atomically, invalid edits keep the last-known-good generation serving. "0" disables reload. Default: 30s.

max_attachment_mb.description: Maximum attachment size in MB for agent runs.

log_level.description: Log level. The --log-level flag takes precedence over this value.

log_format.description: Log format. The --log-format flag takes precedence over this value.

compliance.description: Org-level declared facts used to populate auditor exports (GDPR Art. 30 RoPA, EU AI Act Annex IV). Owned by the platform team together with the DPO. Declared facts only — runtime facts come from the signed evidence store.

name.description: Legal name of the controller organization.

dpo_contact.description: Data Protection Officer contact, where designated.

sovereignty.description: Deployment sovereignty (provider gate, air-gap presets and egress guard). Feature bet 5.3.

mode.description: Single source of truth for data sovereignty. When set it supersedes llm.routing.data_sovereignty_mode and gates providers: eu_strict allows only EU/LOCAL (or EU-region-capable) providers and fails closed on any other declared provider. deployment_mode air_gap implies eu_strict.

deployment_mode.description: air_gap enables eu_strict routing preset, EU/LOCAL egress defaults, and transport-level egress allowlist.

allowed_egress_hosts.description: Optional hosts/URLs added to the air-gap egress allowlist beyond ollama_base_url and gateway providers.

scanner.description: External PII scanner engine (optional). When absent, the built-in regex scanner is used (zero-config default). Selects ONE globally active engine; external engines replace the regex scanner and are fail-closed — an adapter timeout/error blocks egress in enforce mode.

type.description: regex = built-in. presidio = Presidio analyzer REST sidecar (rune offsets by default). http = custom engine speaking the Presidio wire format (byte offsets by default). llm = OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint prompted for NER (e.g. Ollama).

endpoint.description: Engine endpoint: http(s)://host:port or unix:///path/to.sock. Required for presidio/http; for llm defaults to ollama_base_url + /v1. In air_gap deployments only unix sockets, loopback, and private addresses are accepted.

timeout.description: Per-scan deadline (Go duration). No retries; a timeout is an adapter failure and blocks egress in enforce mode.

min_score.description: Entities reported below this confidence are discarded before normalization.

offset_encoding.description: Override for the offset encoding the engine reports. Default depends on type: presidio → rune (stock Presidio reports codepoint offsets), http → byte.

name.description: Detector identity recorded in evidence (defaults to the engine type).

engine_version.description: Operator-declared engine version recorded in evidence (the Presidio /analyze response carries no version).

entities.description: Optional entity-type list forwarded to the engine in each request.

health_check.description: Probe the engine at startup; Talon refuses to start when the probe fails (fail-closed).

model.description: Model id at the OpenAI-compatible endpoint, e.g. llama3.1:8b.

confidence.description: Confidence assigned to LLM-detected entities (LLMs emit no scores).

cache.description: Governed semantic cache (optional, off by default). Validated by `talon doctor`.

ttl_by_tier.description: Optional per-tier TTL overrides in seconds; falls back to default_ttl for tiers not listed.

llm.description: LLM provider and routing configuration (provider registry, EU sovereignty).

type.description: Provider type: openai, anthropic, ollama, bedrock, azure-openai, mistral, etc.

data_sovereignty_mode.description: EU data sovereignty: eu_strict (only EU/LOCAL), eu_preferred (prefer EU, allow fallback), global (no restriction).

pricing_file.description: Path to the LLM pricing table (per-1M-token USD). Used for cost estimation in evidence and OTel.

mode.description: Runtime default when omitted is enforce. Generated starter configs set shadow explicitly for a safe rollout.

dashboard_listen.description: Optional separate bind address for the gateway dashboard (e.g. 127.0.0.1:9091). When empty, dashboard routes are served on the main API server.

network_interception.description: Enterprise DNS interception: route SDK traffic to the gateway without changing app code.

upstream_auth_mode.description: secret reads provider credentials from Talon's vault. client_bearer exists only for the in-process --proxy-quickstart profile and is REJECTED in every YAML-loaded config (#266): a normal gateway's bearer is a Talon agent key and must never be forwarded upstream.

region.description: Jurisdiction of the upstream endpoint (e.g. EU, US, LOCAL). Used by egress rules and data-flow evidence; required for custom base_url providers when region-based egress rules apply.

api_family.description: Wire format of this provider for fallback-chain validation and upstream auth conventions. Defaults by name: 'anthropic' uses the Anthropic Messages API (x-api-key + anthropic-version); everything else is OpenAI-compatible. Set explicitly for aliased endpoints.

responses_store_mode.description: OpenAI Responses API 'store' handling. 'preserve' (default) forwards client intent untouched — an explicit store:false is honored for every client. 'force_if_absent' sets store:true only when the client sent no store field (opt-in for previous_response_id continuity, e.g. OpenClaw). 'force_true' always stores, recording any override of an explicit client store:false in signed evidence (annotation responses_store_overridden). See #213.

inject_stream_usage.description: Add stream_options.include_usage to OpenAI chat-completions streaming requests so the upstream emits a final usage chunk — otherwise streamed chat cost is estimate-only. Default true. Set false to forward the client body unchanged. See #196.

fallback.description: Ordered error-driven fallback chain: on a transient upstream failure (timeout/connection/429/5xx) the gateway retries these targets in order, subject to candidate filters (sovereignty under eu_strict, agent provider allowlists, gateway policy). All targets must share this provider's API family.

provider.description: Name of an enabled gateway provider to fail over to.

model.description: Optional: rewrite the request body's model field for this target.

daily_cost.description: Per-agent daily budget baseline (EUR): the cap an agent runs under unless its own cost_limits replace it. The tighten-only bound is constraints.max_daily_cost.

session_cost.description: Per-session budget baseline (#283): the session soft cap (#198) every agent inherits unless its session_limits.max_cost replaces it. 0/omitted = no default session budget.

defaults.description: Per-agent starting values an agent override may REPLACE (#287). Exceptions keeping their monotonic floor merge: pii_action/response_pii_action (block > redact > warn > allow) and tool_policy_action at the agent layer (an agent cannot loosen block to filter).

allowed_providers.description: Organization-wide HARD constraint (#266): providers ANY agent may reach; an agent's own allowed_providers can only narrow further within this list. Entries must name configured gateway.providers. Empty/omitted = all enabled providers.

allowed_models.description: Organization-wide HARD constraint (#266): model allowlist binding every agent regardless of its override. Empty/omitted = no allowlist constraint.

blocked_models.description: Organization-wide HARD constraint (#266): model blocklist binding every agent regardless of its override ('*' blocks all models).

allowed_tools.description: Organization-wide HARD tool allowlist (#282): when non-empty, a tool must pass this list AND the most-specific allowed list AND every forbidden list. An agent allowlisting a tool the organization never sanctioned does not make it reachable. Empty/omitted = no org allowlist.

forbidden_tools.description: Union with provider and agent forbidden lists — an agent can only add entries, never remove them. Glob patterns supported (e.g. delete_*).

max_daily_cost.description: Organization daily budget CEILING (#287): enforced alongside the resolved per-agent cap, so an agent declaring a bigger budget still denies at the org line (deny reason names the organization). 0/omitted = no ceiling.

max_monthly_cost.description: Organization monthly budget CEILING (#287). 0/omitted = no ceiling.

max_session_cost.description: Organization per-session budget CEILING (#283): an agent session_limits.max_cost above it still denies at the org line. 0/omitted = no ceiling.

max_data_tier.description: Organization-wide data-tier ceiling (#266): 0/public, 1/internal, 2/confidential (named aliases accepted, matching the runtime). An agent's max_data_tier can only LOWER it further.

constraints.description: Organization-wide HARD bounds an agent override can only TIGHTEN within (#287) — none of these are replaced by anything an agent file declares.

scan_tool_content.description: Observation-only PII scan of tool-related request content (tool_use inputs, tool_result outputs, function-call arguments). evidence_only records findings in evidence without influencing enforcement; off disables the scan. Enforcement on tool content is not offered until per-block-type tool redaction exists (#212).

organization_policy.description: Organization policy in two explicit classes (#287): defaults — per-agent baselines an agent override may replace (PII and tool actions keep their monotonic floor merge) — and constraints — organization-wide hard bounds an override can only tighten within. Operational logging/scanning scalars stay at the top level. The pre-split flat keys (default_pii_action, max_daily_cost, allowed_models, ...) are rejected at load with a migration error naming the new location.

response_header_timeout.description: Wait for upstream response headers (time-to-first-byte). Defaults to request_timeout (#230).
